lines.target: Was he at home yesterday? — I think so, but I didn’t phone.
lines.base: ¿Estaba en casa ayer ? — Pienso que sí, pero no llamé por teléfono.
lines.pronunciation: .. dident foun. — 8 ... taiéd... klousd... ais. — 12 ... driimd baat...

lines.target: He called yesterday, but he didn't see me.
lines.base: Vino ayer, pero no me vió. — ¿ Llamó por teléfono ? — No creo.

lines.target: Did he phone? — I don't think so.
lines.base: 6 (Explicación de las formas interrogativa y negativa).

lines.target: You do not change the verb, you simply put the auxiliary “do” into the past
I did not (didn't) like the food. He did not (didn't) phone. We did not (didn't) like him.
Questions are easy, too: Did he like it? Did she phone?
Did you like the play last night? — I didn't see it.
lines.base: ¿Te gustó la obra anoche ? — No la vi.

lines.target: But did you go to the theatre? — Yes but I was so tired I closed my eyes.
lines.base: Pero ¿fuiste al teatro? — Sí, pero estaba tan cansado que cerré los ojos.

lines.target: Tell me, was the play interesting? — I didn't understand very much, it was in Greek.
lines.base: Dime, ¿fué interesante la obra? — No entendí mucho, estaba en griego.

lines.target: Then why did you go? — I liked the main actor
lines.base: Entonces ¿por qué fuiste ? — Me gustaba el actor principal.

lines.target: Did he act well? — I didn't watch the acting. I looked at him,
lines.base: ¿Actuó bien? — No contemplé la actuación. Le miré a él.

lines.target: What did you do after the play? — I dreamed about the actor!
lines.base: ¿Qué hiciste despues de la obra? — ¡Soñé con el actor!

lines.target: He did not, he didn't: He didn't like the play.
Did they...?: Did they phone you?
